images.prompt: A clean closing card. CENTER: a stylized ticket card with the parts clearly labeled and visible: title, description block, three comments with author+timestamp, and a highlighted close-comment-with-rationale at the bottom. BELOW the ticket: a horizontal banner with the bold text "any decision worth remembering = a bw ticket." BELOW THE BANNER: five small companion-skill icons arranged horizontally with their names: 1. compass icon — "beadwork-overview" 2. clock + brain icon — "beadwork-as-memory" 3. network icon — "beadwork-as-bus" 4. magnifying-glass icon — "beadwork-for-meta-analysis" 5. wrench icon — "beadwork-install" BOTTOM: a small caption in italic "open source, MIT-licensed. bw by jallum (github.com/jallum/beadwork)." Caption: "Any decision worth remembering is a ticket." These images are for the beadwork-for-decisions presenter skill — a deep-dive on bw (beadwork — github.com/jallum/beadwork) as decision log / ADR / audit trail substrate. Concrete personas in the scenarios: engineer (architecture decision record), lawyer (privilege log), solo entrepreneur (tax-time decision defense). Colorblind-friendly, no hex codes. Style: flat design, editorial illustration, simple shapes, bold colors on a light warm-gray background. Think 'product strategy deck illustration,' not whiteboard sketch. Generic AI-assistant figures (NOT vendor-branded; do not include any specific agent names like Pliny, Vera, Cato, Ada, or any company logo).
